description: Main entrance opens. Collect your programme booklet and Chalisa card at the welcome desk.
Collective Surya Namaskar — 54 rounds in 6 sets of 9. A moving meditation offered to Hanumanji and the Sun. All levels welcome. Bring a yoga mat.
Registered Havan participants check in at Building 2. Receive your designated seating wristband and ahuti thali information.
All Havan participants assemble and form a sacred procession to the Yajnashala, accompanied by devotional chanting and music.
The sacred Purna Kumbha ritual and ceremonial lamp lighting to consecrate the Yajnashala and invoke the divine presence of Hanumanji.
Vedic opening prayers and Stuti Vandana to set the spiritual tone for the grand Samashti Havan.
Inspirational message and blessings by Pujya Swami Swaroopananda, Global Head of Chinmaya Mission, to inaugurate the grand Vayuputra celebration.

The heart of the event — 1,008 voices chanting the Hanuman Chalisa in a unified Samashti Havan. Multiple sacred fire kunds burn simultaneously. Seating zones: Anjaneya · Bajrang · Chiranjeevi · Dheera.
A vibrant family mela with food vendors, games, crafts and Bala Vihar activities for children of all ages. Community organization and cultural booths open throughout.
Classical dance performances, soul-stirring bhajans, Hanuman Leela drama and Chinmaya Jeevan Darshan — a rich afternoon of devotional arts celebrating the glory of Vāyuputra.
A visual journey into the life, teachings and profound impact of Pujya Gurudev Swami Chinmayananda — celebrating 75 glorious years of Chinmaya Mission.
Refreshments, chai, snacks and a variety of vegetarian food vendors available throughout the day. Prasad distributed after the Havan.
